name: Take Charge America- El Centro
description_short: Take Charge America is a nonprofit financial counseling organization offering free debt management, credit counseling, and housing assistance to consumers struggling with debt.
description_long:
Take Charge America is a nonprofit organization dedicated to providing financial counseling and debt management services to consumers at no upfront cost. The organization operates a national helpline and online counseling platform, with accessibility features and multilingual support (English and Spanish). Their mission centers on empowering individuals to achieve financial freedom through expert guidance and personalized debt solutions.

Take Charge America offers a comprehensive range of services including credit counseling, debt management plans, debt consolidation guidance, debt settlement information, bankruptcy counseling, housing assistance (including foreclosure/eviction prevention), budgeting tools, and financial education. They provide free debt estimation tools that show potential savings through their programs, customizable payment plans, and claim to negotiate reduced interest rates and waived fees with creditors. Consumers can access services through phone counseling (866-750-9618 for new clients, 866-528-0588 for existing clients, Mon-Fri 6am-6pm MST) or online sessions.

The organization distinguishes itself through NFCC certification implied by their nonprofit status and free service model, professional staff with debt management expertise, a 3-5 year average debt repayment timeline, and documented testimonials from satisfied clients across multiple states. They emphasize non-judgmental support, flexibility for life circumstances, and transparent communication. The company also provides educational resources including webinars, money lesson plans, calculators, and a blog covering financial topics relevant to different demographics (millennials, families with children).

However, consumers should understand that Take Charge America is NOT a loan company and does not provide direct lending. Their estimates are based on historical data and actual results vary. The organization operates primarily through debt management plans rather than debt settlement, meaning they work with creditors to restructure existing obligations rather than reduce principal balances. While free, their services require commitment to a structured repayment plan and enrollment of debts with participating creditors.
pros: Completely free financial counseling with no upfront fees, as a certified nonprofit organization
Available 24/5 support via phone (866-750-9618) and online counseling sessions with extended hours (6am-6pm MST)
Free debt estimation tool showing specific savings potential with itemized monthly payments and timeline comparisons
Multilingual support in English and Spanish with accessibility features including font sizing, contrast adjustment, and greyscale options
Claims to negotiate reduced interest rates, waived late fees, and lower monthly payments through debt management plans
Average 3-5 year debt repayment timeline with customizable payment dates
Comprehensive educational resources including webinars, budgeting tools, calculators, and money lesson plans
cons: Not a loan provider—cannot offer emergency cash or direct lending to consumers needing immediate funds
Debt management plans require creditor cooperation; not all creditors may participate or agree to reduced rates
Estimates are explicitly based on historical data; actual savings and timelines vary significantly by individual situation and creditors enrolled
Requires commitment to a structured multi-year repayment plan, limiting flexibility if financial circumstances change
Primary service is debt management restructuring rather than debt settlement or principal reduction
best_for: Consumers with $5,000+ in unsecured debt (credit cards, personal loans) seeking structured repayment without taking new loans
Individuals facing collection calls or late payment issues who want to negotiate with creditors through professional intermediaries
Homeowners at risk of foreclosure or renters facing eviction who need housing counseling and financial guidance
First-time homebuyers or savers needing budgeting education and credit improvement strategies
diagnosis: Take Charge America is best for consumers with moderate to high unsecured debt ($5,000+) who want professional, free guidance to restructure payments and negotiate with creditors, but are not seeking emergency loans or debt settlement. The primary caveat is that actual results depend entirely on creditor participation and cooperation—not all creditors will accept reduced rates or waived fees, and consumers must commit to a multi-year structured repayment plan.
services: Free credit counseling and financial assessments
Debt management plans with creditor negotiation
Bankruptcy pre-filing counseling and certificate programs
Free debt estimation calculator with savings projections
Budgeting tools and financial planning resources
Money lesson plans for financial literacy
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 20430 N 19Th Ave, Suite B155, Phoenix, AZ 85027
